inline_comments.message:
Hey, sorry if it's a stupid question, but where do you find this image ? I don't see a 1.7.0 tag on sdc-docker-base 😕
And subsequent question for anyone out there, am I right in guessing that https://jenkins.onap.org/view/sdc/job/sdc-master-verify-java/configure doesn't build the docker images ? I don't see the docker profile being specified

This is the actual very good question :) This image should be built by the maven in the https://gerrit.onap.org/r/admin/repos/sdc/sdc-docker-base repository.

Like you can see in the comment under review: 
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/sdc/sdc-docker-base/+/108763
(changes that provide python3 for uses by this commit).

Currently, the image is produced with tag 1.6.1-SNAPSHOT-20200615T080732Z. So this should be adjusted.

I got an error when running this locally, seems this is not defined if i'm not mistaken :
      File "/home/onap/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/sdcBePy-1.7-py3.8.egg/sdcBePy/users/run.py", line 9, in <module>
        from sdcBePy.common.healthCheck import check_backend, RETRY_ATTEMPTS
    ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'sdcBePy.common.healthCheck'
done in the other related reviews
